mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate crossing above the 9.5 percent threshold signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in greater than 55 percent of instances following a UNRATE rise above the 9.5 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand recovery, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint RSAFS and HOUST expansions fail to predict positive 20-day returns with a statistical significance level of p less than 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production reflects immediate manufacturing and utility output expansion, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during early recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth shows zero predictive power for 5-day returns during recovery periods, with a mean directional accuracy below 50 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent nonfarm payroll growth exceeding 100,000 and a declining unemployment rate confirms genuine labor market tightening and wage growth expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases greater than 100k while UNRATE declines
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal yields negative 20-day returns in greater than 60 percent of triggered observations over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation crossing above a 0.3 percent monthly growth threshold forces expectations of tighter monetary policy and higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL crosses above 0.3 percent monthly growth threshold
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in greater than 50 percent of cases following CPIAUCSL acceleration above the specified threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product confirms broad economic expansion and corporate earnings momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows no statistically significant positive correlation with 5-day forward returns over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Synchronized increases in industrial production and nonfarm payrolls indicate robust capacity utilization and employment-driven output growth, resulting in positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint INDPRO and PAYEMS signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold baseline over 20-day horizons in out-of-sample validation.
